apiConfig.description: Employee Leave Action Details API - fetches leave actions for date range

testCases.title: Validate JSON response structure for https://portal.zinghr.com/2015/route/LMS/EmployeeLeaveActionDetails
testCases.description: Validate that POST request returns JSON with 3 expected fields: Code, Message, EmployeeLeaveTransactions

steps.action: Prepare valid request with all required parameters
Verify field data types match expected schema

assertions: Response contains field "Code" of type number
Response contains field "Message" of type string
Response contains field "EmployeeLeaveTransactions" of type array
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper

def response = context.response
def jsonSlurper = new JsonSlurper()
def jsonRoot = jsonSlurper.parseText(response.contentAsString)
def json = jsonRoot

assert response.statusCode == 200 : "Expected 200 but got " + response.statusCode
assert json.Code instanceof Number : "Code must be a number"
assert json.Message instanceof String : "Message must be a string"
assert json.EmployeeLeaveTransactions instanceof List : "EmployeeLeaveTransactions must be an array"
assert response.responseTime < 2000 : "Response time exceeded 2s"

testCases.title: Verify response schema validation for https://portal.zinghr.com/2015/route/LMS/EmployeeLeaveActionDetails
testCases.description: Validate that response body matches the expected JSON schema
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response
def json = new groovy.json.JsonSlurper().parseText(response.contentAsString)

assert json.id instanceof Number : "id must be a number"
assert json.status instanceof String : "status must be a string"

testCases.title: Verify 400 error for invalid request body
testCases.description: Validate proper error handling when request body contains invalid data
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response

assert response.statusCode == 400 : "Expected 400 but got " + response.statusCode
def json = new groovy.json.JsonSlurper().parseText(response.contentAsString)
assert json.error != null : "Error message should be present"

testCases.title: Verify 401 error for unauthorized access
testCases.description: Validate that requests without valid authentication return 401 Unauthorized
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response

assert response.statusCode == 401 : "Expected 401 but got " + response.statusCode
assert response.contentAsString.toLowerCase().contains("unauthorized")

testCases.title: Verify SQL injection prevention
testCases.description: Validate that API properly sanitizes input to prevent SQL injection attacks

steps.action: Send request with injection in query param

testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response
def body = response.contentAsString.toLowerCase()

assert !body.contains("sql") : "SQL error exposed"
assert !body.contains("syntax error") : "Syntax error exposed"
assert response.statusCode != 500 : "Server error occurred"

testCases.title: Verify XSS prevention in response
testCases.description: Validate that API properly escapes output to prevent XSS attacks
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response
def body = response.contentAsString

assert !body.contains("<script>") : "Script tag not escaped"
assert !body.contains("onerror=") : "Event handler not escaped"

testCases.title: Verify handling of empty request body
testCases.description: Validate proper handling when request body is empty
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response

assert response.statusCode == 400 : "Should return 400 for empty body"
def json = new groovy.json.JsonSlurper().parseText(response.contentAsString)
assert json.error != null || json.message != null

testCases.title: Verify maximum payload size handling
testCases.description: Validate API behavior with very large request payloads
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response

assert response.statusCode in [400, 413] : "Should reject large payload"
assert response.statusCode != 500 : "Server should remain stable"

testCases.title: Verify response time under normal load
testCases.description: Validate that API response time is within acceptable limits
testCases.readyApiGroovy:
def response = context.response

assert response.responseTime < 2000 : "Response time exceeded 2000ms"
assert response.statusCode == 200 : "Request should succeed"
